from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    # Templates for natural‑language rendering of predicates.
    # {subj} and {obj} will be substituted with the subject and object strings.
    predicate_templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "{subj} has the {obj} architectural style",
        "buildingStartDate": "Construction of {subj} began in {obj}",
        "completionDate": "{subj} was completed in {obj}",
        "floorCount": "{subj} has {obj} floors",
        "location": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "cost": "{subj} costs {obj}",
        "floorArea": "{subj} covers a floor area of {obj}",
        "architect": "The architect of {subj} is {obj}",
        "owner": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
        "formerName": "{subj} was formerly known as {obj}",
        "height": "The height of {subj} is {obj}",
        "buildingType": "{subj} is a {obj}",
        "developer": "{subj} was developed by {obj}",
        "tenant": "{subj} has a tenant {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
        "country": "{subj} is in {obj}",
        "currentTenants": "{subj} currently houses {obj}",
        "address": "{subj} has the address {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "{subj} was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "{subj} was born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "{subj} died in {obj}",
        "bedCount": "{subj} has {obj} beds",
        "region": "{subj} is in the region of {obj}",
        "state": "{subj} is in the state of {obj}",
        "website": "{subj} has website {obj}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "{subj} was constructed in {obj}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "{subj} has NRHP reference number {obj}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "{subj} was added to the NRHP on {obj}",
        "significantBuilding": "{subj} is known for the building {obj}",
        "governingBody": "{subj} is governed by {obj}",
        "leader": "The leader of {subj} is {obj}",
        "chancellor": "{subj} has chancellor {obj}",
        "governmentType": "{subj} has government type {obj}",
        "capital": "The capital of {subj} is {obj}",
        "language": "{obj} is the language of {subj}",
        "leaderTitle": "The leader title of {subj} is {obj}",
        "currency": "{subj} uses currency {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{obj} people are an ethnic group in {subj}",
        "origin": "{subj} originates from {obj}",
        "significantProject": "{subj} is known for the project {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "{subj} was founded in {obj}",
        "demonym": "The demonym of {subj} is {obj}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{subj} has {obj} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "{subj} has key person {obj}",
        "architecture": "{subj} features architecture {obj}",
        "postalCode": "{subj} has postal code {obj}",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        parts = []
        handled = set()

        # Helper to clean quoted objects
        def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
            return o.strip('"').strip("'")

        # Special predicates that require inverted phrasing or refined wording
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate == "capital":
                parts.append(f"The capital of {subject} is {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "language":
                parts.append(f"{clean_obj(tr.object)} is the language of {subject}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "buildingStartDate":
                parts.append(f"Construction of {subject} began in {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "inaugurationDate":
                parts.append(f"The inauguration of {subject} was on {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "leader":
                # Prefer “The leader of X is Y.” phrasing for higher BLEU alignment.
                parts.append(f"The leader of {subject} is {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "leaderTitle":
                parts.append(f"The leader title of {subject} is {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            # New refined handling
            elif tr.predicate == "architect":
                # Invert to “The architect X designed Y.”
                parts.append(f"The architect {clean_obj(tr.object)} designed {subject}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "height":
                # Produce “The height of Y is Z metres.”
                # Clean numeric part and optional unit
                obj_clean = clean_obj(tr.object)
                # Remove parentheses and possible unit words, keep numeric value
                import re
                num_match = re.search(r"[\d\.]+", obj_clean)
                unit_match = re.search(r"(metre|meter|metres|meters)", obj_clean, re.IGNORECASE)
                value = num_match.group(0) if num_match else obj_clean
                unit = unit_match.group(0).lower() if unit_match else "metres"
                parts.append(f"The height of {subject} is {value} {unit}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "demonym":
                # “The demonym of X is Y.”
                parts.append(f"The demonym of {subject} is {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)

        # Preferred ordering of common attributes for readability.
        order = ["location", "height", "architect", "buildingType", "completionDate"]
        for pred in order:
            if pred in handled:
                continue
            for tr in group:
                if tr.predicate == pred:
                    verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
                    clause = f"{verb} {clean_obj(tr.object)}"
                    parts.append(clause)
                    handled.add(pred)
                    break

        # Add remaining predicates that were not handled above.
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in handled:
                continue
            verb = verb_map.get(tr.predicate, "has")
            obj = clean_obj(tr.object)
            if tr.predicate in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} floors"
            else:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
            parts.append(clause)

        # Build the sentence for this subject.
        if parts:
            # Determine if any part already mentions the subject, in which case we avoid prefixing it again.
            subject_mentioned = any(subject in part for part in parts)
            if subject_mentioned:
                sentence = ", ".join(parts) + "."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts) + "."
        else:
            sentence = subject + "."

        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine sentences for multiple subjects.
    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    # Capitalize the first character of the whole output.
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
